MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brendan Tuohy of K & L Gates (249.1077) for defendant and moving party
Jon Neumann and Selby Lighthill of Paul & Hanley (510.559.9980) for plaintiff and responding party

Mr. Neumann made an oral request for 473(b) relief, which the Court denied. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CRANE CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR #4780

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jon Neumann and Selby Lighthill of Paul & Hanley (510.559.9980) for plaintiff and responding party
David Mann of Foley & Mansfield (510.590.9500) for defendant and moving party

Argued. Defendant's request for a 437 (c)(h) continuance to obtain additional deposition testimony and supplement briefs was denied. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT FLUOR CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Central to the motion is the scope of the complaint. Defendant may be correct that the complaint does not specifically allege para-occupational exposure. However, in plaintiffs' special interrogatory responses, plaintiffs' set forth this theory and the basis for it. As a consequence, defendant was on notice and given those circumstances, the Court may grant leave to amend to conform to proof. As a consequence, defendant did not shift the burden of proof and the declarations of Gosney and Hatfield also create a triable issue of fact on the issue of exposure. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. An order on defendant's evidentiary objections was signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR #4780

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jon Neumann and Selby Lighthill of Paul & Hanley (510.559.9980) for plaintiff and responding party
Patrick Malone of McKenna Long & Aldridge (267.4000) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Defendant has not shifted the burden of proof based on the testimony of Rodger Harris at 727-732. Even if the burden shifted, the declarations of Mr. Gosney and Mr. Hatfield create a triable issue of fact on exposure. Defendant's reliance on the general state all facts interrogatory is not sufficient to shift the burden of proof on the issues of summary adjudication because the focus of the question is exposure, not the other issues. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Evidentiary objections were signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR #4780
